name: Prison Simulator VR
developer: PlayWay S.A., Driment Studio, Baked Games SA
short_description: Step into the world of Prison Simulator VR to assume the role of a prison guard, managing the complex inner workings behind bars and uncovering secrets of this cutthroat world. Respect is a gain given only to those with a strong hand, but what kind of a guard you'll be depends only on you!
detailed_description:
Don't miss your chance to become a herald of the law or a corrupted mole in cahoots with the prisoners, all from the comfort of your own home! Step into the world of Prison Simulator VR to assume the role of a prison guard, managing the complex inner workings behind bars and uncovering secrets of this cutthroat world. Respect is a gain given only to those with a strong hand, but what kind of a guard you'll be depends only on you!

In Prison Simulator VR, you become the master of your own experience. There's a freedom in what you do, regardless if it is to be a vigilant watchman of order or turn a blind eye to the prisoners' nefarious deals.

With a wide variety of different tasks, search prison cells, confiscate illegal items, interact with the seemingly bleak prison environment and its tenants, instilling fear or gaining respect - your choices matter! Each and every character will react to the things you do, but whether you end up with more friends or enemies is entirely up to you.

Watch out! You never know what might happen if you push them a little too hard!

On top of it all, Prison Simulator VR has two modes available, depending on your preference. You can explore the story mode, following thrilling clues and twists as you uncover what lurks within. For players who prefer a gameplay with less restraints, freeplay mode is a perfect way to live the every day life of a guard without the constraints of a plot.

supported_languages:
English*, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Simplified Chinese, Polish, Russian, Turkish, Portuguese - Brazil
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text: I've played the game for about 1.5 hours and it's good and if you likes the flatscreen version of the game I think the $10 is worth it. It does have a little bit of a learning curve to it. But I would recommend it.

[h1]Experienced on the Meta Quest 3[/h1]

You can view my gameplay & impressions here: https://youtu.be/wE8WXs7KQD8

For the budget price, this is a surprisingly good game. I was not expecting much. I would say this isn't the perfect VR port of the pancake version, but it's a pretty good port for the price. I was surprised by the the depth behind the game.

First of all, game looks good. It's not the best looking PCVR game, but it looks better than the the vast majority of Steam VR releases that look like Quest 2/3 ports. While I wouldn't say the game is smooth & polished, it's also not a jank fest like most PCVR releases these days. It was totally functional game. There's stuff that can be improved, such as the interactions / dialogues with the prisoners. Also, putting back the searched boxes was a bit more finnicky than I expected. 

Game is running on the Unity Engine. On my RTX 5090, I was getting a smooth 90 fps. I did not experience any serious issues or bugs. You do have full locomotion & smooth turning available. You can play comfortably seated (you have button crouch & height adjustment). There's limited graphical settings, but at least they are there.

[b]Rate 7/10.[/b]

[quote]Please note: I received a free Steam key through the Steam Curator Connect program. If you enjoyed my review, please consider joining my Steam Curator group [url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/Oculus_WMR]Oculus Rift Reviews.[/url][/quote]

Bad game. Super confusing can't find my way around the game and when I do get to where it tells me to go nothing happens. Also super annoying that the voice acting isn't english idk what language it is but it's annoying listening to it. over all 1/10
never wrote a review for a game but this is not good at all
